concerns.text: The 'Feature Branch' field contains an unfilled template placeholder '[###-cosmic-ray-anisotropy-solar-cycle]'. This is a structural defect preventing the spec from being actionable or version-controlled. The branch name must be resolved to a valid slug before the spec can advance.

FR-006 defines a 'positive result' label requiring |r| ≥ 0.4 AND p ≤ 0.0017 for BOTH detectors independently. The Idea's 'Expected results' section states success if the condition is met for 'at least one proxy–detector pair'. This is a scope drift (redefinition of success criteria) that makes the spec stricter than the research goal, potentially causing a valid scientific finding to be labeled a 'negative result' by the system.

SC-006 requires the report to state if the hypothesis is supported at '≥ 3 σ confidence (defined as p ≤ 0.00135)'. This contradicts FR-006 and SC-004/SC-005 which use a Bonferroni-corrected threshold of α = 0.0017. The spec contains internal contradictions on the definition of statistical significance, which will lead to ambiguous reporting logic.
